{"notes":"Phase 1 findings:

1. Contentlayer DOES regenerate all 6 documents on every build (confirmed by file timestamps changing between builds)
2. However, the contentlayer build takes only ~2.7 seconds standalone
3. Full build times: cold (no caches) ~79s, warm (with .next and .contentlayer caches) ~48s
4. Webpack compilation dominates: 46s cold, 18s warm
5. With only 5 blog posts + 1 author doc, contentlayer processing is negligible (~2.7s / ~48s = ~5.6% of warm build)
6. The onSuccess callback (tag-data.json, search.json) runs every time but is fast
7. Vercel has no special cache config (empty vercel.json)
8. .contentlayer/ is gitignored so Vercel cold-builds it each deploy, but it's still only ~2.7s

Conclusion: The problem exists (docs ARE rebuilt every time) but the impact is negligible at current scale (5 blog posts). Phase 2 optimization would save ~2.7s per build — not worth the complexity."}
